description:
You are a newly-hired system-administrator who has to produce a daily security report that counts
    the number of failed SSH login attempts recorded on the server.  
    The previous admin has already archived three daily log files under  
    
        /home/user/logs/  
            ├── auth.log.2023-07-17  
            ├── auth.log.2023-07-18  
            └── auth.log.2023-07-19  
    
    1. Write a set of terminal commands that inspects every file whose name matches the glob  
          /home/user/logs/auth.log.20*  
       and extracts **only** the lines that represent *failed* SSH logins.  
       • A line is considered a failed SSH login if it matches the regular expression  
         ^.*sshd\[.*\]:\ Failed\ password  
         (Use the literal spaces shown above. Everything before “sshd[” and everything after the words
         “Failed password” can vary.)  
       • You may assume the log lines follow the canonical OpenSSH syslog format.  
       • Process the three log files concurrently (e.g. with xargs -P, GNU parallel, “&” in the background, or
         any other safe method).  The exact mechanism is up to you, but you must actually run the
         commands in parallel instead of strictly one after another.

    2. From the matching lines, build a daily summary that shows how many failed attempts occurred
       on each day represented by the files.  
       • The day must be taken **exclusively** from the date component embedded in the **file
         name** (the YYYY-MM-DD portion that comes after “auth.log.”), not from the timestamp that
         appears at the start of each individual log line.  
       • Create the directory  
            /home/user/output  
         if it does not already exist, and make sure it is readable and traversable by everyone  
         (mode 0755 is fine).  

    3. Save the summary to the file  
          /home/user/output/ssh_fail_summary.log  

       The file must be world-readable (mode 0644 is enough) and must contain **exactly three**
       non-blank lines, one per day, sorted in ascending date order.  
       The format of each line is strictly:  

           YYYY-MM-DD,COUNT

       • There must be **no** leading or trailing spaces on any line.  
       • Lines must end with a single Unix newline character “\n”.  
       • Example of the *shape* (do not copy, your numbers must come from the data):  
           2025-12-31,17

    4. When you are done, you can cat the summary file to show its contents, but **do not** manually
       edit it; the counts must be generated via your command pipeline.

    The automated tests will verify:
       • existence and permissions of /home/user/output/ssh_fail_summary.log  
       • that the file contains exactly the three correctly-sorted lines in the exact format described  
       • that the counts are correct according to the supplied log files  
       • that no extra blank lines or spaces appear anywhere in the file
truth:
<!--
    Pre-existing artefacts
-->
<preexisting>
    <directory path="/home/user/logs" mode="0755"/>
    <file path="/home/user/logs/auth.log.2023-07-17" mode="0644"><![CDATA[
Jul 17 02:15:43 server sshd[12345]: Failed password for invalid user admin from 192.168.1.5 port 53422 ssh2
Jul 17 04:20:11 server sshd[12346]: Accepted password for user1 from 10.0.0.3 port 41414 ssh2
Jul 17 14:55:01 server sshd[12347]: Failed password for root from 203.0.113.8 port 62119 ssh2
Jul 17 18:37:29 server sshd[12348]: Failed password for invalid user test from 198.51.100.23 port 33788 ssh2
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/logs/auth.log.2023-07-18" mode="0644"><![CDATA[
Jul 18 03:05:13 server sshd[22345]: Failed password for root from 198.51.100.23 port 42312 ssh2
Jul 18 05:30:55 server sshd[22346]: Failed password for invalid user guest from 203.0.113.8 port 61311 ssh2
Jul 18 05:31:02 server sshd[22347]: Failed password for invalid user guest from 203.0.113.8 port 61312 ssh2
Jul 18 10:01:11 server sshd[22348]: Accepted password for user2 from 10.0.0.4 port 33899 ssh2
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/logs/auth.log.2023-07-19" mode="0644"><![CDATA[
Jul 19 07:42:00 server sshd[32345]: Failed password for invalid user admin from 192.168.1.5 port 52013 ssh2
Jul 19 07:42:09 server sshd[32346]: Failed password for invalid user admin from 192.168.1.5 port 52014 ssh2
Jul 19 09:15:00 server sshd[32347]: Failed password for root from 198.51.100.23 port 63321 ssh2
Jul 19 15:21:42 server sshd[32348]: Failed password for invalid user test from 203.0.113.8 port 31114 ssh2
Jul 19 20:01:17 server sshd[32349]: Accepted password for user3 from 10.0.0.5 port 22555 ssh2
]]></file>
</preexisting>

<!--
    Expected artefacts after the agent finishes
-->
<expected>
    <directory path="/home/user/output" mode="0755"/>
    <file path="/home/user/output/ssh_fail_summary.log" mode="0644"><![CDATA[
2023-07-17,3
2023-07-18,3
2023-07-19,4
]]></file>
</expected>
